rxn_class_summary: Visible-light triplet energy transfer enabled intermolecular [2π+2σ] cycloaddition of aryl/heteroaryl ketoesters (benzoylformate esters) with bicyclobutanes (BCBs) to directly assemble polysubstituted 2-oxabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes via a biradical/aryl-migration cascade

key_intermediates.name: Triplet excited benzoylformate ester (1,2-diradical, species A)
1,5-diradical from addition of excited ketoester onto BCB (species B)
Closed-shell oxa-bicyclic intermediate after ISC and radical-radical coupling (species C)
Backbone C-H abstracted carbon radical (species D)
Bridged aryl-migration transition state / cyclohexadienyl-type intermediate (species E)
Stabilized α-ester benzyl-type radical after aryl migration (species F)
Norrish type II product (off-pathway, from o-Me substrate 3r)

electron_flow: Ir-F photocatalyst is excited by visible light and transfers triplet energy to the benzoylformate ester, generating a 1,2-(C,O)-diradical (A); this triplet diradical adds across the central C–C σ-bond of the BCB to give the most stabilized 1,5-diradical (B), which after ISC closes by radical–radical coupling to the 2-oxa-BCH (C). A second excited ketoester then abstracts the backbone C–H to give radical D, which undergoes aryl 1,2-migration through cyclohexadienyl intermediate E to the more stable benzyl radical F, finally quenched by HAT from an H-donor.
rate_determining_step: Addition of the triplet excited benzoylformate ester onto the BCB to form the 1,5-diradical B is the regioselectivity-determining (and likely rate-determining) step

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction suppressed; TEMPO-adducts detected by ESI-HRMS, supporting a stepwise radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Styrene (alternative radical acceptor in place of ketoester)
probe_reagents.observation: Under 365 nm or standard conditions with BCB 2a, no cycloadduct detected, indicating BCBs do not initiate the radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: Direct 365 nm UV excitation (no photocatalyst)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a + 2a still gave 3a in 50% yield, consistent with sensitization of the ketoester triplet

probe_reagents.name: o-Methyl benzoylformate ester (substrate probe for Norrish II)
probe_reagents.observation: Gave Norrish type II product 7 (39%) rather than cycloadduct 3r (n.d.), demonstrating accessible triplet ketone biradical reactivity

probe_reagents.name: Stern–Volmer quenching with 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Both substrates quench excited *[IrF] at similar rates

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a and 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Redox potentials outside the range of *[IrF], ruling out single-electron transfer

probe_reagents.name: UV/vis of 1a + 2a mixture
probe_reagents.observation: No evidence for EDA complex formation, supporting energy-transfer mechanism

notes: Authors favor an energy-transfer (triplet sensitization) mechanism over single-electron transfer or EDA-complex pathways, based on Stern–Volmer, CV and UV/vis data. Regioselectivity is rationalized by formation of the most stabilized 1,5-diradical B and by the aryl-migration step that yields the more stabilized benzyl radical F; a reversible HAT step accounts for selective C–H abstraction at the backbone position. Substrate scope (only disubstituted BCBs; o-Me ketoester gives Norrish II 7) and X-ray structures (3a, 3h, 3q, 3v, 3z, 6) support the proposed connectivity. Mechanism explicitly noted by the authors as 'remains to be elucidated'.
